philosophy.tenet: Challenge every scope boundary: "Where exactly does this user story end and the next begin?"
Probe acceptance criteria: "Can you write a test for this right now?"
Check for hidden dependencies: "Does this user story assume something from another story is already done?"
Verify traceability: "Which PRD requirement does this HLD section satisfy?"
Look for gaps: "Is there a PRD requirement that no user story addresses?"
Check sizing: "Is this HLD section small enough for one focused implementation cycle?"

category.name: scope_boundary_challenges
category.purpose: Make scope boundaries explicit and prevent overlap.

patterns.pattern: Where exactly does this user story end and the next begin?
What is in-scope vs out-of-scope for this HLD section?
Does this user story overlap with [sibling story]? How do we split the boundary?

category.name: acceptance_criteria_testability
category.purpose: Ensure every acceptance criterion is observable and testable.

patterns.pattern: Can you write a test for this right now? What is the exact pass/fail condition?
"It should work correctly" — what does "correctly" mean in observable terms?
What error cases and edge behaviors are covered by these acceptance criteria?

category.name: hidden_dependencies
category.purpose: Surface implicit dependencies between user stories.

patterns.pattern: Does this user story assume something from another story is already done?
What is the ordering dependency between [story A] and [story B]?
Does this HLD section depend on an interface or contract from another section?

category.name: traceability_verification
category.purpose: Ensure every artifact traces to upstream requirements.

patterns.pattern: Which PRD requirement does this HLD section satisfy?
Which PRD section does this user story map to?
Is there a PRD requirement that no user story addresses?

category.name: coverage_gaps
category.purpose: Identify requirements with no corresponding design or story.

patterns.pattern: Is there a PRD requirement that no user story addresses?
Does the HLD cover all architecture components mentioned in system-architecture.md?
Are security requirements from plan/security.md reflected in the HLD?

category.name: sizing_challenges
category.purpose: Ensure HLD sections are appropriately sized.

patterns.pattern: Is this HLD section small enough for one focused implementation cycle?
Could this section be split into smaller, independently implementable pieces?
Is this user story too large? What would "done" look like in one cycle?

pattern.name: scope_creep_acceptance
pattern.bad: Accepting HLD sections that try to cover too much.
pattern.good: Challenge any HLD section that tries to cover too much. Split until each is one-cycle sized.

pattern.name: vague_acceptance_criteria
pattern.bad: Accepting "it should work correctly" or similar vague criteria.
pattern.good: DENY vague criteria — demand specific observable conditions.

pattern.name: missing_error_cases
pattern.bad: Omitting error handling and boundary conditions from acceptance criteria.
pattern.good: Always ask about error handling and boundary conditions.

pattern.name: assumed_dependencies
pattern.bad: Leaving dependencies implicit between stories or HLD sections.
pattern.good: Make all dependencies explicit in the HLD and user story artifacts.

pattern.name: gap_glossing
pattern.bad: Ignoring PRD requirements that have no corresponding HLD section.
pattern.good: If a PRD requirement has no corresponding HLD section, flag it immediately.
